metadata.name: RelAIEO verification norms
metadata.description: OUR verification layer (RQ3) expressed in RelAIEO's own vocabulary: each norm references RelAIEO concept IDs. Because RelAIEO is a reflective, open-world auditing ontology (not a punitive rule set), an activated harm-risk combined with a build/deploy intent (the 'design' concept) is ESCALATED for human review rather than hard-denied, and the RelAIEO rdfs:provocation of the triggering concept is surfaced as the review prompt. Hard blocks stay in the rule-based layer (policies/core_policy.json).

norms.description: Designing/deploying a system that performs surveillance or tracking of people.
norms.rationale: RelAIEO frames surveillance as an identified harm risk of AI systems; building one warrants reflective human audit.

norms.description: Designing/deploying a system that harvests or exposes personal data.
norms.rationale: RelAIEO frames threats to privacy as an identified harm risk; building a system around personal-data extraction warrants reflective audit.

norms.description: Designing/deploying a system that produces or spreads information disorder (mis/disinformation, deepfakes, propaganda).
norms.rationale: RelAIEO frames information disorder as an identified harm risk; building such a system warrants reflective audit.

norms.description: Designing/deploying a system intended to deskill or replace human workers.
norms.rationale: RelAIEO frames deskilling as an identified harm risk; the socio-technical impact warrants reflective audit rather than an automatic yes/no.

norms.description: Designing/deploying a system that encodes or reproduces bias/discrimination.
norms.rationale: RelAIEO frames bias as an identified harm risk rooted in structural inequality; building such a system warrants reflective audit.

norms.description: Signals of ethics washing (presenting a system as ethical for marketing/PR).
norms.rationale: RelAIEO models ethics washing as a form of ethics manipulation; flag for accountability review.
